summary.intent: Fix a clippy lint error (manual_div_ceil) in the telegram crate, then investigate and fix a recurring install script bug (issue #496) where .deb filenames had a spurious -1 revision suffix, add regression tests, and ship a new release.
summary.outcome: All three lint fixes were committed and pushed (div_ceil, install.sh deb naming, topic.rs unwrap_used in tests), a new check script with 13 assertions was added to both CI and local-validate.sh, and release 20260328.01 was tagged and pushed.

repo: cargo-deb with --deb-version produces filenames without a revision suffix (moltis_VERSION_ARCH.deb), while cargo-generate-rpm and the Arch packaging workflow both produce filenames with -1 revision (moltis-VERSION-1.ARCH.rpm, moltis-VERSION-1-ARCH.pkg.tar.zst).
install.sh and website/install.sh are kept identical; .polyphony/ copies are untracked and not part of the repo.
The workspace-level clippy config denies both unwrap_used and expect_used everywhere including tests; test modules must add #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)] explicitly.
CI already ran check-website-install-sync.sh before this session; the new check-install-package-names.sh is additive.
The release workflow uses cargo deb ... --deb-version "$MOLTIS_VERSION" with no --deb-revision flag, which is the root cause of the -1 mismatch.
Version scheme is date-based (20260327.05) for releases; release_tag() outputs bare string for date-based and v-prefixed for semver.

code.finding: Manual div_ceil pattern (x + 2) / 3 replaced with MAX_INLINE_DOCUMENT_BYTES.div_ceil(3) per clippy::manual_div_ceil lint.
deb_file constructed with -1 revision suffix but cargo-deb --deb-version produces no revision suffix; removed -1 from both install.sh and website/install.sh.
Test functions used .unwrap() which is denied by workspace clippy config; fixed by adding #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)] on the test module.
Three spots needed nightly rustfmt reformatting (assert_eq! line length, Arc construction, multi-arg assert_eq!).
New script with 13 checks validating filename patterns for all package types against the release workflow; macOS grep does not support -P (PCRE) so the script uses plain ERE patterns.
Added check-install-package-names.sh as a CI step.
Added install-names check as a parallel fast check alongside fmt/biome/i18n/zizmor.

workflow: local-validate.sh E2E tests are flaky (clearChatAndWait timeouts, shiki highlighter timeouts); retrying is the correct response.
macOS grep lacks PCRE (-P flag); use ERE patterns with -E instead when writing portable shell scripts.
Sourcing install.sh and intercepting download/sudo calls is fragile for testing; grepping for filename patterns directly is more robust.
A rebase was needed before the final push of the install fix, indicating concurrent activity on main.

friction: E2E tests are flaky (required 3 attempts for local-validate.sh to pass clean).
macOS grep PCRE incompatibility caused the first version of check-install-package-names.sh to fail and required a rewrite.
The first approach to testing install scripts (sourcing and intercepting) was abandoned as too fragile.
A rebase was required mid-session due to concurrent pushes to main.
The previous fix for issue #493 fixed the tag URL but missed the -1 suffix bug, showing the fix was incomplete.
open_items: Monitor the release 20260328.01 CI workflow to confirm all packages (deb, rpm, arch, binary tarballs, macOS app) build and publish successfully.
Issue #496 was fixed but not explicitly closed in the transcript — may need to be closed manually.
The flaky E2E tests (clearChatAndWait, shiki highlighter timeout) are a recurring issue not addressed in this session.
